Empires & Puzzles Francine Review: 5-Star Legendary Nature Green Hero

Francine was once the queen of Halloween with her fast cleanse and shock therapy. But with stats from 2020, does this Frankenstein's monster still have a spark, or is she just dead weight? We review the base hero.
Francine: The Shocking Cleanse That Aged Like Milk?
Francine: The Shocking Therapist Who Cleaned Up (In 2020)

Francine, the "Monstress" of the Morlovia family, burst onto the scene in Halloween 2020 as a fast-mana Nature Rogue. Back then, she was a breath of fresh air—or perhaps a jolt of fresh electricity—offering a unique recurring cleanse mechanic that made her a staple in many rosters.

. While she has received a costume that significantly alters her power level (which we'll discuss later), we're here to see if the original model still holds a charge in today's high-voltage meta. Spoiler alert: She might need a jump start.

Francine's design is a delightful homage to the classic Bride of Frankenstein, but with a "medical chic" twist. She sports the iconic streaked hair and stitches, but trades the wedding gown for bandages and a pair of menacing scissors. It's a clean, sharp look that screams "I'm going to fix you, whether you like it or not." Compared to her Mafia-themed costume, the base art feels more rooted in classic horror, which purists might prefer—even if her stats are scarier than her face these days.
Francine's base stats are severely outdated.

With a base power of around 795 (and ~951 even when Limit Broken twice), she is miles behind modern heroes who regularly push past 1250 base power and 1500+ when fully maxed. Her attack and defense values, which were once considered "solid," now look like 4-star stats in a 5-star world. She is extremely fragile by 2026 standards and will struggle to survive even a glancing blow from modern snipers.
The core of Francine's kit is her speed and her cleanse. At
is... okay. It wasn't earth-shattering in 2020, and today it's essentially a "tickle" to modern tanks.

. Unlike a standard one-time cleanse, Francine applies a buff that cleanses all allies at the end of each turn for 3 turns. This is fantastic against damage-over-time (DoT) dealers or ailment-heavy teams, as it effectively "scrubs" your team clean repeatedly. However, on the base version, this buff is
. In an era where dispel is common and buffs are stolen constantly, this limits her reliability.
is a nice flavor text that makes her hit slightly harder against Blue enemies, but given her low base attack stat, don't expect her to one-shot anyone.
Let's crunch the data to see what we're really working with.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The Morlovia family bonus offers a Mana Generation boost (+5%/+12%). This is excellent for ensuring your team fires faster, but you need to run her with other Halloween heroes (like Alucard or Miriam & Midnight) to get the most out of it.

This was added later in her lifecycle and is arguably her best feature now. A 65% chance to steal a dispellable buff is strong utility. If there are no buffs to steal, she deals a little extra damage (150% to all), which helps pad her low damage output.
A flat boost to attack for the first 6 turns. While helpful, it doesn't solve her fundamental issue of low base stats.
In late 2020, Francine was a top-tier flank. Her recurring cleanse was a hard counter to the "GTV" (Gravemaker, Telluria, Vela) meta that relied heavily on DoT.

Power creep has hit her like a truck. Her stats are simply too low to be competitive in Diamond arena raids or top-tier wars. She will die before she fires, and when she does fire, the damage is negligible.
The base version of Francine is largely a relic. She might find use in War Clean-up teams or mid-level tournaments, but she cannot compete with modern 5-stars.

Sabina, Melendor, or any modern dispeller will wipe her cleanse buff immediately.
Any modern sniper (e.g., Jwala, Turundh) will likely one-shot her.

If you pulled Francine early in your journey, congrats! She will serve you well through the map stages and lower-level raids. Her cleanse is a mechanic that teaches you the importance of timing. However, do not over-invest in her limit breaks unless you have her costume.
. Without the costume, she has no place in a veteran's roster. Even with the costume, she is a niche Titan hero or specific counter. Do not waste Alpha Aethers on the base version.
"A green GM or Clarissa with better damage skill multiplier... I look forward to this hero."
- The community correctly identifying the true green queen of 2020.
"She is a top tier hero with huge stats."
- A quote that has aged poorly, showing just how fast the game moves.

Base Francine is a reminder of simpler times. She was a good therapist for our rosters back when a little recurring cleanse was all we needed to feel better. Today, she's sadly retired. If you have her costume, she puts on a new suit and gets back to work. If not, she's best left enjoying her retirement on your bench.
A deep dive into Francine, the fast mana Nature cleanser from the Morlovia family. We analyze her viability in the modern meta, her unique 3-turn cleanse, and whether she's worth your tonics today.
